title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Calgary continues his discussion with Dr. McMaster regarding the murder of Mrs. Aigle. While no obvious motive is apparent, McMaster suggests that Mrs. Aigle maintained an intense economic and psychological grip on her non-biological children, stifling their autonomy. McMaster details the backgrounds of several potential suspects: Mike, a resentful car salesman who harbored hatred for both his biological and adoptive mothers; Hester, an unsuccessful actress who was forced to return home; and Mary Dalton, who faced pressure from her mother after marrying a man against her wishes. McMaster also mentions a Swedish masseuse who disliked Mrs. Aigle and Leo Aigle, who is planning to remarry a compatible woman.

McMaster agrees with the police assessment that a family member likely committed the crime, but he expresses fear that the truth will remain hidden due to a lack of evidence. He compares the situation to the historical Bravo case, noting that when a murderer escapes justice, the innocent are often left to suffer under the weight of suspicion.

In London, Hester Aigle is startled by Colston Lindstrom. Colston remarks on the "scary things" present in houses and recounts a newspaper story about a woman who killed her companion while under the delusion of exorcising a devil. He cryptically warns Hester that because the contents of a twisted mind are unknowable, he might one day decide to kill her mother.
